directives.title: Download Pre-made Design System Markdown File
steps: Navigate to the specified resource location (e.g., a website with design.md files).
Locate and click the designated link or button to initiate the download of a `design.md` file.
Save the downloaded `design.md` file to the local filesystem.
directives.rationale: The speaker explicitly states, 'You click here, you download it, and it it contains all of this visual stuff, typography, colors, spacing.' This describes a clear, sequential action to acquire a specific file.

directives.title: Attach Design System Markdown File to AI Prompt
steps: Ensure a `design.md` file containing the desired design system (e.g., typography, colors, spacing definitions) is readily accessible.
Access the AI model's prompt interface or API endpoint.
Utilize the platform's mechanism to attach the `design.md` file as part of the prompt submission.
Submit the prompt to the AI model, including the `design.md` file as an attachment.
directives.rationale: The speaker instructs, '...you put it all into it an MD file and then you put it into your prompt as an attachment and then boom, you have a beautiful design.' This outlines a direct action of file attachment to an AI prompt.

directives.title: Inspect Design System Markdown File Structure
steps: Open the `design.md` file using a markdown-compatible text editor or viewer.
Identify and list the top-level markdown elements such as titles (headers).
Locate and analyze any structured data presented within markdown tables.
Identify and review any embedded code blocks within the file.
Document how visual design elements (e.g., typography, colors, spacing) are represented and organized within the markdown structure.
directives.rationale: The speaker describes the `design.md` markdown as 'essentially a structured text with tables, uh titles, and code.' This provides concrete elements to look for when inspecting the file's structure.

directives.title: Provide Foundational Design System to AI Agent for Consistency
steps: Locate a `design.md` file that defines a foundational design system, including elements such as process, typography, colors, spacing, and component definitions.
Ingest the *entire* content of the located `design.md` file as input to the AI agent.
Instruct the AI agent to utilize this foundational design system to ensure consistency when generating new designs or iterating on existing ones.
directives.rationale: The speaker explicitly advocates for `design.md` as a 'foundational system' to provide to an AI agent to achieve 'consistency across the board' and avoid 'design drift,' directly contrasting it with providing 'something too specific.' This is a clear, actionable directive for how an AI agent should interact with design specifications.

directives.title: Prepare UI Screens/Sections from Local Content via Agent
steps: Organize all relevant content (e.g., a podcast transcript, design brief, or data for sections) into local Markdown (`.md`) files. Ensure these files are structured across appropriate folders and nested folders on the local file system.
Open the root folder containing these organized `.md` files within the 'Codex' application.

Issue a command to the AI agent (integrated with or accessible via Codex) to process the content. For example, 'Look, I have a great a Greg Eisenberg uh podcast. I want you to prepare all of these screens.' or 'I want you to prepare, you know, 10 sections' based on the content in the opened local `.md` files.
directives.target: Local File System, Codex Application, AI Agent System
directives.rationale: This directive reconstructs a direct, multi-step workflow described by the speaker, involving local file generation, interaction with a specific tool (Codex), and issuing explicit commands to an AI agent for content preparation. The example ('Greg Eisenberg podcast', 'prepare screens/10 sections') provides concrete context for execution.

directives.title: Instruct AI Agent to Remember Design Memory
steps: Ensure that the `design.md` file contains the desired design principles, patterns, or 'secret sauce' that should be part of the system's design memory.

Issue an explicit command to the AI agent to commit the contents of this `design.md` file to its internal memory. Examples of such commands include 'Remember this [design.md]', 'Remember that [design.md]', or 'Remember the workflow you just done [referring to design principles documented in `design.md`]'.
directives.rationale: The speaker explicitly describes a process of storing design information in `design.md` and then instructing an AI agent to 'remember' it for cross-platform and consistent application. This directly translates into an actionable directive for an agent.

directives.title: Instruct AI Agent to Generate Content from Local MD Files
steps: Locate the local folder containing the MD files (e.g., generated from a podcast transcript).
Open this folder within the Codex or similar AI agent environment.
Issue an instruction to the AI agent to 'prepare all of these screens' based on the content of the MD files.
Issue an instruction to the AI agent to 'prepare 10 sections or 10 notes' based on the content of the MD files.
Issue an instruction to the AI agent to 'do research' based on the content of the MD files.
Confirm the agent generates all requested output files (screens, sections, notes, research) in a single operation.
directives.target: Local MD files, AI Agent system (e.g., Codex, OpenClaw, CloudCode)
directives.rationale: The speaker explicitly states the actions to give to an AI agent, the input (MD files), and the expected outputs (screens, sections, notes, research), providing a clear procedural sequence for an autonomous agent with the specified capabilities.

directives.title: Remix Design Elements on Variant.com for Customization
steps: Navigate to variant.com.
Access the 'community' section of the website.
Browse the available 'skills' or design elements.
Select a desired 'skill' or design element.
Click the 'remix' button associated with the selected element to apply it.
Repeat the remixing process until the design flow is satisfactory.
Once satisfied, identify and copy the prompt associated with the chosen skill or final design.
directives.rationale: The steps explicitly describe interactions with a specific web platform (variant.com) to customize design elements, including clear actions like navigating, clicking 'remix', and copying a 'prompt'.

directives.title: Download HTML and Design MD Files from Template
steps: Navigate to the page of a chosen design template (e.g., on variant.com or similar platform).
Locate the download options for the template.
Initiate the download of the HTML file associated with the template.
Initiate the download of the design.md file associated with the template.
directives.rationale: The directive explicitly names the files (HTML and design.md) and the action (download), providing a fully clear and actionable sequence for a web automation agent.

directives.title: Download Template Design Assets
steps: Navigate to a chosen template's source or page.
Download the HTML file associated with the template.
Download the `design.md` file associated with the template.
directives.target: Local file system (e.g., a project's assets directory)
directives.rationale: The directive explicitly states which files to download (HTML and design.md) and implies a source (a template), making it highly specific and executable by a file management agent.

directives.title: Prepare AI Prompt with Design Context and Request
steps: Obtain the content of the downloaded `design.md` file.
Obtain the content of the downloaded HTML file (if available and required for context).
Construct an AI prompt that includes these file contents as contextual input.

Append a specific request for generating a landing page, including details about the startup (e.g., 'create a landing page for my startup called Aura, a chat app that is using AI and that ships stuff to people to their email').
directives.rationale: This sequence outlines how to feed design knowledge into an AI model as context and then make a specific generation request, including an example. It's a clear process for AI interaction.

directives.title: Configure AI Generation Tool for Web Output
steps: Access the AI generation platform or tool's user interface/API.
Locate and select the 'web' option for the desired output type.
Locate and select '3.1' as a specific version or configuration parameter for the generation process.
directives.target: AI Generation Platform's User Interface or API Parameters
directives.rationale: The directive provides concrete, distinct configuration choices ('web', '3.1') that an agent can apply within a specific AI generation tool's settings, making it an implementable UI/API interaction.

directives.title: Generate Design Footer Component
steps: Issue a command to the design generation tool to create a footer component.
Verify the footer element has been successfully added to the active design.
directives.target: Design generation tool / UI component system
directives.rationale: The speaker explicitly states the action 'create the footer' and the expected outcome of adding it to the design.

directives.title: Apply Specific Design Skill (e.g., 'Laser') via Prompt Interface
steps: Navigate to the 'prompt' interface of the 'new form' tool.
Select the 'Laser' skill/setting from the available options.
Initiate the design generation process to apply the 'Laser' skill to the current design.
Optionally, queue additional design generations with this or other skills for concurrent processing.
directives.rationale: This describes a sequence of UI interactions to select and apply a named skill, followed by the option to queue more.

directives.title: Convert Current Design to Mobile Layout
steps: Navigate to the 'mobile' conversion feature or view within the design tool.
Initiate the conversion process to adapt the current design for a mobile layout.
Verify the design displays correctly and responsively on a mobile viewport.
directives.rationale: The speaker clearly indicates the action 'turn something into mobile' by 'going to mobile' and seeing the design converted.

directives.title: Concurrent Multi-Asset Design Generation
steps: Submit a request to the AI design system for a 'slide deck' generation.
Submit a request to the AI design system for a 'motion design' generation.
Submit a request to the AI design system for a 'hero section' generation.
Ensure these generation tasks are executed concurrently by the system.
Monitor and retrieve the generated 'slide deck', 'motion design', and 'hero section' assets.
directives.rationale: The speaker outlines requesting multiple distinct design assets ('slide deck', 'motion design', 'hero section') and performing them 'at the same time,' indicating concurrent execution.

directives.title: Utilize Skeuomorphic Design Skill
steps: Retrieve the prompt string associated with the 'skeuomorphic design' skill from the skill repository.
Submit the retrieved prompt to the active AI agent as a direct instruction.
Direct the AI agent to generate a design output that embodies the skeuomorphic style based on the provided prompt.
directives.rationale: This sequence details how to programmatically invoke a specific design style by retrieving a predefined skill prompt and instructing an AI agent to use it for design generation.

directives.title: Position 3D Globe Element Over Text
steps: Identify the unique identifier or reference for the 3D globe element that has been generated.
Identify the unique identifier or reference for the target text element within the design canvas.
Adjust the Z-index and/or spatial coordinates of the 3D globe element to render it visually 'on top of' the target text element within the design canvas.
directives.target: Design Canvas / UI Rendering Engine / Scene Graph
directives.rationale: This provides explicit, actionable instructions for manipulating the display order and position of specific design elements to achieve a desired visual hierarchy.

directives.title: Extract Specific Design Components from Skill
steps: Access the data structure or definition of a specified design skill.
From the skill's data, parse and extract the defined color palette (e.g., a list of hex codes, RGB values, or named colors).
From the skill's data, parse and extract the defined typography settings (e.g., font families, sizes, weights, line heights, font smoothing properties).
Make the extracted color and typography components available for further programmatic use (e.g., storing in variables, copying to a clipboard, applying to a new design context).
directives.rationale: This outlines a precise procedure for programmatically extracting reusable design parameters like colors and typography from a skill's definition, implying they are stored in a machine-readable format.
